sections.paragraph:
στρατοπεδάρχης (στρατόπεδον, ἀρχή; Dionys. Hal. 10, 36; Lucian, Hist. 22; Vett. Val. 76, 13; Jos., Bell. 2, 531; 6, 238; OGI 605, 3; BGU 1822, 13 [I b.c.]; Mitt-Wilck. II/2, 87, 5 [II a.d.]) so t.r. Ac 28:16 (s. Tdf.) or στρατοπέδαρχος, ου, ὁ military commander, commandant of a camp Ac 28:16 v.l. On the subject-matter s. Mommsen and Harnack, SBBerlAk 1895, 491ff; Zahn, Einl.3 I 392ff; AWikenhauser, Die AG 1921, 358f; Pauly-W. IV 1896ff; Haenchen ad loc.; Mason 87; HTajra, The Trial of St. Paul ’89, 177.—M-M. TW.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

AG AG = Apostelgeschichte (in German titles relating to Acts)

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Mason Mason = HMason, Greek Terms for Roman Institutions—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 948
